summary: Dependency-confusion beacon exfiltrates host identity on npm install
details: On `npm install`, the package's preinstall hook (`node index.js > /dev/null 2>&1`) collects the installer's username, current working directory, Node.js version, platform, architecture, and a generated UUID, then POSTs them as JSON to https://avamnrwqo7.rbmock.dev/ (a third-party mock-API host unrelated to any plausible publisher). stdout/stderr are redirected to /dev/null so the beacon is silent during install. The package has no main module (declared entry is absent from the tarball), an empty description, vanity version 2.999.0, and placeholder author 'poc' — it provides no functionality beyond the install-time callback. The name 'backoffice-charges-module' is the shape used for dependency-confusion attacks targeting an internal package name that a victim's build pipeline may resolve from the public registry.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
